function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    if (N === 0) return 0;

    let total_good_subarrays = 0;
    
    // Map to store the leftmost starting index L for each unique OR value O ending at j-1.
    // {OR_value: leftmost_start_index}
    let current_or_map = new Map(); 

    // Map to track the rightmost index p <= j where nums[p] == V
    // {Value: rightmost_index}
    let last_occurrence = new Map();

    for (let j = 0; j < N; j++) {
        const current_val = nums[j];
        
        // --- 1. Update OR Map (Transition from j-1 to j) ---
        const next_or_map = new Map();

        // 1a. Subarray [j, j]
        next_or_map.set(current_val, j);

        // 1b. Extend previous subarrays ending at j-1
        for (const [o_prime, l_prime] of current_or_map.entries()) {
            const O = o_prime | current_val;
            
            // We want to store the minimum starting index L_prime for the OR value O.
            // Since we are processing previous structures, L_prime is a candidate minimum start index.
            if (!next_or_map.has(O) || l_prime < next_or_map.get(O)) {
                next_or_map.set(O, l_prime);
            }
        }

        current_or_map = next_or_map;

        // --- 2. Count Good Subarrays ending at j ---
        
        // Convert map entries to an array and sort by start index (L) descendingly.
        // This helps in defining the contiguous ranges R_k = [L_{k+1}+1, L_k].
        const entries = Array.from(current_or_map.entries());
        entries.sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]); // Sort by index L (the second element) descendingly

        // Get the rightmost occurrence of current_val (nums[j])
        // Since we update last_occurrence at the end of the loop, we use the value before update.
        // For O = current_val, p_max is at least j.
        
        for (let k = 0; k < entries.length; k++) {
            const [O, L] = entries[k];
            
            // Determine the range R = [L_next + 1, L]
            // L_next is the start index of the next block (k+1), or -1 if k is the last block.
            const L_next = (k + 1 < entries.length) ? entries[k+1][1] : -1;
            const L_start = L_next + 1;
            
            // Find p_max: the largest index p <= j such that nums[p] == O.
            let p_max;
            if (last_occurrence.has(O)) {
                p_max = last_occurrence.get(O);
            } else {
                // Should not happen if O was generated correctly, unless O=0 and 0 never appeared before.
                // Since O must be generated by numbers present in nums[0..j], if O != 0, it must exist.
                // If O=0, it only exists if nums[i]=0 for some i.
                // Given the logic, if O is in current_or_map, it must be achievable, and if O != 0, it must be present.
                // If O=0, we must check if 0 occurred.
                p_max = -1; 
            }

            // Check if the condition holds for any i in [L_start, L].
            // The count is the size of the intersection: [L_start, L] intersected with [0, p_max].
            // Intersection range: [L_start, min(L, p_max)]
            
            const effective_end = Math.min(L, p_max);
            
            if (effective_end >= L_start) {
                const count = effective_end - L_start + 1;
                total_good_subarrays += count;
            }
        }

        // --- 3. Update LastOccurrence for the next iteration j+1 ---
        last_occurrence.set(current_val, j);
    }

    return total_good_subarrays;
}

```javascript
function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    if (N === 0) return 0;

    let total_good_subarrays = 0;
    
    // Map to store the leftmost starting index L for each unique OR value O ending at j-1.
    // {OR_value: leftmost_start_index}
    let current_or_map = new Map(); 

    // Map to track the rightmost index p <= j where nums[p] == V
    // {Value: rightmost_index}
    let last_occurrence = new Map();

    for (let j = 0; j < N; j++) {
        const current_val = nums[j];
        
        // --- 1. Update OR Map (Transition from j-1 to j) ---
        const next_or_map = new Map();

        // 1a. Subarray [j, j]
        next_or_map.set(current_val, j);

        // 1b. Extend previous subarrays ending at j-1
        for (const [o_prime, l_prime] of current_or_map.entries()) {
            const O = o_prime | current_val;
            
            // We want to store the minimum starting index L_prime for the OR value O.
            // Since we are processing previous structures, L_prime is a candidate minimum start index.
            if (!next_or_map.has(O) || l_prime < next_or_map.get(O)) {
                next_or_map.set(O, l_prime);
            }
        }

        current_or_map = next_or_map;

        // --- 2. Count Good Subarrays ending at j ---
        
        // Convert map entries to an array and sort by start index (L) descendingly.
        // This helps in defining the contiguous ranges R_k = [L_{k+1}+1, L_k].
        const entries = Array.from(current_or_map.entries());
        entries.sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]); // Sort by index L (the second element) descendingly

        // Get the rightmost occurrence of current_val (nums[j])
        // Since we update last_occurrence at the end of the loop, we use the value before update.
        // For O = current_val, p_max is at least j.
        
        for (let k = 0; k < entries.length; k++) {
            const [O, L] = entries[k];
            
            // Determine the range R = [L_next + 1, L]
            // L_next is the start index of the next block (k+1), or -1 if k is the last block.
            const L_next = (k + 1 < entries.length) ? entries[k+1][1] : -1;
            const L_start = L_next + 1;
            
            // Find p_max: the largest index p <= j such that nums[p] == O.
            let p_max;
            if (last_occurrence.has(O)) {
                p_max = last_occurrence.get(O);
            } else {
                // Should not happen if O was generated correctly, unless O=0 and 0 never appeared before.
                // Since O must be generated by numbers present in nums[0..j], if O != 0, it must exist.
                // If O=0, it only exists if nums[i]=0 for some i.
                // Given the logic, if O is in current_or_map, it must be achievable, and if O != 0, it must be present.
                // If O=0, we must check if 0 occurred.
                p_max = -1; 
            }

            // Check if the condition holds for any i in [L_start, L].
            // The count is the size of the intersection: [L_start, L] intersected with [0, p_max].
            // Intersection range: [L_start, min(L, p_max)]
            
            const effective_end = Math.min(L, p_max);
            
            if (effective_end >= L_start) {
                const count = effective_end - L_start + 1;
                total_good_subarrays += count;
            }
        }

        // --- 3. Update LastOccurrence for the next iteration j+1 ---
        last_occurrence.set(current_val, j);
    }

    return total_good_subarrays;
}
```

```javascript
function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    if (N === 0) return 0;

    let total_good_subarrays = 0;
    
    // Map to store the leftmost starting index L for each unique OR value O ending at j-1.
    // {OR_value: leftmost_start_index}
    let current_or_map = new Map(); 

    // Map to track the rightmost index p <= j where nums[p] == V
    // {Value: rightmost_index}
    let last_occurrence = new Map();

    for (let j = 0; j < N; j++) {
        const current_val = nums[j];
        
        // --- 1. Update OR Map (Transition from j-1 to j) ---
        const next_or_map = new Map();

        // 1a. Subarray [j, j]
        next_or_map.set(current_val, j);

        // 1b. Extend previous subarrays ending at j-1
        for (const [o_prime, l_prime] of current_or_map.entries()) {
            const O = o_prime | current_val;
            
            // We want to store the minimum starting index L_prime for the OR value O.
            // Since we are processing previous structures, L_prime is a candidate minimum start index.
            if (!next_or_map.has(O) || l_prime < next_or_map.get(O)) {
                next_or_map.set(O, l_prime);
            }
        }

        current_or_map = next_or_map;

        // --- 2. Count Good Subarrays ending at j ---
        
        // Convert map entries to an array and sort by start index (L) descendingly.
        // This helps in defining the contiguous ranges R_k = [L_{k+1}+1, L_k].
        const entries = Array.from(current_or_map.entries());
        entries.sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]); // Sort by index L (the second element) descendingly

        // Get the rightmost occurrence of current_val (nums[j])
        // Since we update last_occurrence at the end of the loop, we use the value before update.
        // For O = current_val, p_max is at least j.
        
        for (let k = 0; k < entries.length; k++) {
            const [O, L] = entries[k];
            
            // Determine the range R = [L_next + 1, L]
            // L_next is the start index of the next block (k+1), or -1 if k is the last block.
            const L_next = (k + 1 < entries.length) ? entries[k+1][1] : -1;
            const L_start = L_next + 1;
            
            // Find p_max: the largest index p <= j such that nums[p] == O.
            let p_max;
            if (last_occurrence.has(O)) {
                p_max = last_occurrence.get(O);
            } else {
                // Should not happen if O was generated correctly, unless O=0 and 0 never appeared before.
                // Since O must be generated by numbers present in nums[0..j], if O != 0, it must exist.
                // If O=0, it only exists if nums[i]=0 for some i.
                // Given the logic, if O is in current_or_map, it must be achievable, and if O != 0, it must be present.
                // If O=0, we must check if 0 occurred.
                p_max = -1; 
            }

            // Check if the condition holds for any i in [L_start, L].
            // The count is the size of the intersection: [L_start, L] intersected with [0, p_max].
            // Intersection range: [L_start, min(L, p_max)]
            
            const effective_end = Math.min(L, p_max);
            
            if (effective_end >= L_start) {
                const count = effective_end - L_start + 1;
                total_good_subarrays += count;
            }
        }

        // --- 3. Update LastOccurrence for the next iteration j+1 ---
        last_occurrence.set(current_val, j);
    }

    return total_good_subarrays;
}
